claim: Auditory entrainment is strengthened when people see congruent visual and auditory information.
evidence_sentences: UNLABELLED Congruent audiovisual speech enhances our ability to comprehend a speaker, even in noise-free conditions.

When incongruent auditory and visual information is presented concurrently, it can hinder a listener's perception and even cause him or her to perceive information that was not presented in either modality.
We demonstrate that the cortical representation of the speech envelope is enhanced by the presentation of congruent audiovisual speech in noise-free conditions.
Finally, our data suggest that neural entrainment to the speech envelope is inhibited when the auditory and visual streams are incongruent both temporally and contextually.
SIGNIFICANCE STATEMENT Seeing a speaker's face as he or she talks can greatly help in understanding what the speaker is saying.
Specifically, we show that the brain's representation of auditory speech is enhanced when the accompanying visual speech signal shares the same timing.
